title: 「While = Although」句型
titleEn: Using While as Although to express concession and contrast

omissions: The Important Rules and Benefits explanations are provided in Chinese only; their English fields are faithful editorial translations.
The PDF title has no separate English subtitle; titleEn is an editorial translation.

formula: While + subject + verb + other information, subject + verb + other information

formulas.formula: While + subject + verb + other information, subject + verb + other information

example: While natural foods are nutritious, they are often too expensive.
exampleZh: 雖然天然食品營養豐富，但價格往往太高。

examples.en: While natural foods are nutritious, they are often too expensive.
examples.zh: 雖然天然食品營養豐富，但價格往往太高。
examples.highlight: While natural foods are nutritious, they are often too expensive.

zh: 在這個句型中，while 與 although 意思相同，用來先承認一項事實，再提出另一項與它形成對比或出乎意料的事實。

rules.en: In this lesson, While means “although”, not “during the time that”.
rules.zh: While 在本課表示「雖然」，不是「當……的時候」。

rules.en: Add a comma after a While clause when it comes at the beginning of a sentence.
rules.zh: 放在句首的 While clause 後面要加逗號。

rules.en: Both sides of the comma must be complete clauses, normally each containing a subject and a verb.
rules.zh: 逗號前後都必須是完整的子句，通常各自包含主語和動詞。

rules.en: Do not add but before the main clause after using While.
rules.zh: 使用 While 後，主句前不需要再加 but。
examples: Correct: While natural foods are nutritious, they are often expensive.
Incorrect: While natural foods are nutritious, but they are often expensive.

rules.en: Pronouns must agree in number with the nouns they refer to.
rules.zh: 代名詞必須與所指的名詞保持單複數一致。

benefits.en: It connects two opposing ideas naturally. You do not need to split the information into several short sentences, so the expression becomes smoother.
benefits.zh: 把兩個相反的想法自然連接起來。不需要把資料拆成幾個短句，表達會更流暢。

benefits.en: It makes speaking and writing sound more balanced. You can first acknowledge the other side before presenting your main point, so your view does not sound overly one-sided.
benefits.zh: 讓說話和寫作顯得更平衡。你可以先承認另一面的事實，再表達主要觀點，不會令人覺得你的看法過於片面。

benefits.en: It is suitable for essays, discussions and spoken answers. The structure can compare advantages and disadvantages, ideals and reality, and different people’s views.
benefits.zh: 適合用於作文、討論和口語回答。這個句型可以用來比較優點與缺點、理想與現實，以及不同人的看法。

benefits.en: It reduces repeated use of but and although. Learning while gives you more sentence variety and makes your English sound more natural and mature.
benefits.zh: 減少重複使用 but 和 although。學會使用 while，可以令句式更多變，英文聽起來更自然和成熟。

sourceOmissions: The Important Rules and Benefits explanations are provided in Chinese only; their English fields are faithful editorial translations.
The PDF title has no separate English subtitle; titleEn is an editorial translation.

en: Rewrite each sentence using While to express concession or contrast.
The first word of each answer has been provided.
zh: 使用 While 改寫以下句子，以表達「雖然……但……」的讓步或對比關係。
每題已提供答案的第一個字。

questions.prompt: Although Mia is tired, she is finishing her homework.
questions.promptZh: 雖然米婭很疲倦，但她正在完成家課。
questions.answer: While Mia is tired, she is finishing her homework.
questions.answerZh: 雖然米婭很疲倦，但她正在完成家課。
questions.highlight: While Mia is tired, she is finishing her homework.

questions.prompt: The bag is small, but it can hold many books.
questions.promptZh: 這個袋很小，但它可以裝很多本書。
questions.answer: While the bag is small, it can hold many books.

questions.answer: While the product is environmentally friendly, its high price may discourage some buyers.
